explanation: The requirement is that Start-up Mode should only be initiated by pressing the power button, but only after the system has been connected to (1) the breathing circuit (without patient), (2) the air supply, and (3) the power source. Start-up Mode should not start unless all these prerequisites are fulfilled and the power button is pressed. The most fitting LTL template is a safety property (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)), which ensures that whenever the conjunction of prerequisites and the power button press occurs, Start-up Mode is entered. Here, bool_exp1 is the AND of all connection requirements and the power button press; bool_exp3 is StartUpMode. N_DURATION is not required (null) for this template.

The natural language requirement specifies that Start-up Mode ('StartUpMode') must only be initiated after the system is connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source (without patient connection), and by pressing the power button. Thus, Start-up Mode should only be entered if all of 'breathingCircuitConnected', 'airSupplyConnected', and 'powerConnected' are true and 'powerButton' is pressed. To capture this, we use the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', where bool_exp1 should represent the conjunction of all these preconditions and the power button press, and bool_exp3 should be 'StartUpMode'. This guarantees that whenever the power button is pressed and all connections are made (with no patient connected), the system transitions to Start-up Mode. We choose N_DURATION = 3 (arbitrary > 0, as no explicit duration is specified).

The natural language requirement states that 'Start-up Mode' should only be initiated (i.e., become active) after the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power, and after the power button is pressed. This is best captured by ensuring that 'StartUpMode' is only true if all the preconditions ('powerConnected', 'breathingCircuitConnected', and 'airSupplyConnected') are true and the 'powerButton' is pressed. The temporal pattern to capture 'StartUpMode' initiation upon pressing the power button after all conditions are satisfied is naturally written as: globally, if the conjunction of connections and the power button being pressed occurs, this leads to 'StartUpMode'. This matches a safety-style pattern, best represented by the template: 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)', where bool_exp1 and bool_exp2 can be combined as multiple enabling conditions. I use their conjunction for that purpose, and the result expresses that globally, only when all the prerequisites (connections and power button press) are met does 'StartUpMode' become active.

The requirement specifies that Start-up Mode (which initializes the system for operation) should only be initiated when all prerequisite connections are made (breathing circuit, air supply, and power source) and when the power button is pressed. This means the transition to Start-up Mode is enabled by the logical combination: powerConnected AND airSupplyConnected AND NOT patientConnected AND powerButton. These ensure the system is not yet connected to a patient, but has all required non-patient connections, and the user has pressed the button. The LTL template G((A & B) -> C) captures a necessary condition for an event (here, entering StartUpMode) in terms of other events/conditions, and enforces the requirement globally. Therefore, 'Globally, if the breathing circuit is connected (powerConnected), the air supply is connected (airSupplyConnected), the patient is not connected (¬patientConnected), and the power button is pressed (powerButton), then StartUpMode is initiated (StartUpMode)'. N_DURATION is not needed here, so is set to null.

The requirement states that the Start-up Mode shall be initiated (i.e., StartUpMode becomes true) as a result of pressing the power button (powerButton) after the system is connected to the breathing circuit (breathingCircuitConnected), the air supply (airSupplyConnected), and the power source (powerConnected). Thus, whenever all three connections are present and the power button is pressed, Start-up Mode should be entered. This causal relationship is best expressed using a global implication template: whenever all preconditions (connections and button press) are met, StartUpMode holds. By using the conjunctive condition as the antecedent, the formula ensures the system enters StartUpMode only after all connections and the button press are true. This strictly models the procedural context described in the natural language and rationale.

The natural language asserts that Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) should be initiated only after the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, the air supply, and the power source. This means that pressing the power button (powerButton) should only lead to StartUpMode if (powerConnected AND airSupplyConnected AND breathingCircuitConnected) are all true, and the patient is NOT connected (as per the requirement 'without connection to the patient'). Therefore, the property expresses that always (globally), if all three connections are made and the patient is NOT connected, and the power button is pressed, then the StartUpMode should be initiated in the next state. This fits template 41.

The natural language states the Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) should only be initiated (i.e., become true) after the system is connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power source, and then when the power button is pressed. From the atomic propositions, 'powerConnected' can be reasonably interpreted as connected to both the breathing circuit and the power source (none exist for air supply, so we'll assume 'powerConnected' includes all necessary connections). We require: (1) StartUpMode can only be entered if powerButton is pressed AND the system is already connected (powerConnected & powerButton), AND (2) it should not be possible to enter StartUpMode before these conditions. This is captured as: whenever StartUpMode becomes true, it must be the case that (powerConnected & powerButton) is true at that moment. This is classically represented by the safety template G(StartUpMode -> (powerConnected & powerButton)).

The requirement states that Start-up Mode (StartUpMode) should only be initiated (i.e., true) once the system is connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power source, and the power button is pressed. We represent this as: StartUpMode can only become true if all connections are attached (breathingCircuitConnected, airSupplyConnected, and powerConnected) and the power button is pressed (powerButton). The template 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)' requires that whenever all preconditions (all connections + powerButton) are met, then StartUpMode is initiated. Thus, this matches the described requirement. N_DURATION is not needed here (set to null).

The property requires that whenever the system has been connected to the breathing circuit, air supply, and power source, and then the power button is pushed, then Start-up Mode shall be initiated. This captures the sense that the Start-up Mode should only be initiated after proper connections are done and a power button press occurs, following the intent to ensure proper system readiness by operator action. The template '(G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3))' is used, with bool_exp1 covering all preconditions (connection to breathing circuit, air supply, power source) and bool_exp2 indicating the power button is pressed, while bool_exp3 expresses that StartUpMode is initiated. N_DURATION is not relevant for this template.

The requirement specifies that the Start-up Mode ('StartUpMode') should be initiated (i.e., become true) only after the system is connected to the breathing circuit ('breathingCircuitConnected'), the air supply ('airSupplyConnected'), and the power source ('powerConnected'), and after pressing the power button ('powerButton'). To capture this, the property must ensure StartUpMode is never enabled until all the conditions above are true and the power button is pressed. In LTL, this is best expressed as: 'Globally, if StartUpMode is active, then breathingCircuitConnected, airSupplyConnected, powerConnected, and powerButton must all be true'. This means StartUpMode cannot be active unless all conditions have been met. This is captured by template 30 with: G((precondition) -> StartUpMode), where precondition = (breathingCircuitConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton).
